papers.title: Uneven Evolution of Cognition Across Generations of Generative AI Models
papers.reason: Excluded because it evaluates the cognitive abilities of generative AI models rather than human participants.

papers.title: ASR-Agnostic Multimodal Spectrotemporal Modeling for Early Dementia Detection
papers.reason: It addresses human dementia detection from speech but lacks an interactive decision task, eye tracking, qualifying EEG analysis, or a cognitive process model.

papers.title: Demystifying Video Reasoning
papers.reason: Excluded because it studies reasoning and working memory inside diffusion-based video generation models.

papers.title: The Richest Paradigm You're Not Using: Commercial Videogames at the Intersection of Human-Computer Interaction and Cognitive Science
papers.reason: It centrally proposes interactive commercial games with behavioral timing and eye tracking for studying human executive function, but it is a perspective without an empirical participant study.

papers.title: Pupillometry and Brain Dynamics for Cognitive Load in Working Memory
papers.reason: Human participants perform an interactive digit-span task while pupil dynamics and EEG are analyzed, although the described EEG features do not establish frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Quant VideoGen: Auto-Regressive Long Video Generation via 2-Bit KV-Cache Quantization
papers.reason: Excluded because it concerns video generation and the memory and quantization of a model's KV cache.

papers.title: Cognitive Load Estimation Using Brain Foundation Models and Interpretability for BCIs
papers.reason: It performs human EEG-based cognitive-load monitoring, but the summary provides neither an interactive task nor qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity methodology.

papers.title: Cross-Modal Computational Model of Brain-Heart Interactions via HRV and EEG Feature
papers.reason: It uses a human working-memory task, but its EEG contribution is limited to spectral band-power and generic features while its synthetic generator is not a cognitive decision-process model.

papers.title: Unveiling the Heart-Brain Connection: An Analysis of ECG in Cognitive Performance
papers.reason: It includes an active human working-memory paradigm, but EEG is represented through spectral features rather than detailed frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Your Eyes Controlled the Game: Real-Time Cognitive Training Adaptation based on Eye-Tracking and Physiological Data in Virtual Reality
papers.reason: It combines interactive adaptive VR tasks targeting attention and working memory with centrally analyzed real-time eye-tracking data from human participants.

papers.title: How Light Shapes Memory: Beta Synchrony in the Temporal-Parietal Cortex Predicts Cognitive Ergonomics for BCI Applications
papers.reason: It analyzes beta-phase EEG connectivity across cortical regions while participants perform interactive working-memory recall and sequence tasks.

papers.title: Setting up for failure: automatic discovery of the neural mechanisms of cognitive errors
papers.reason: Excluded because its neural validation uses macaque rather than human neural data, so the study population is not restricted to human participants.

papers.title: A Quantitative Framework for Assessing Sleep Quality from EEG Time Series in Complex Dynamic Systems
papers.reason: It combines a human working-memory task with EEG phase-amplitude coupling, frequency-power, and connectivity analyses used to derive an electrophysiological marker.

papers.title: Quantifying the Effects of Word Length, Frequency, and Predictability on Dyslexia
papers.reason: Eye tracking is central to comparing naturalistic reading behavior in dyslexic and typical human readers, but the reading condition is not an interactive decision task.

papers.title: Working Memory Functional Connectivity Analysis for Dementia Classification using EEG
papers.reason: Human clinical groups perform working-memory tasks while EEG functional connectivity and network metrics are analyzed for AD, MCI, and control classification.

papers.title: Bayesian Distributional Models of Executive Functioning
papers.reason: Excluded because the evaluation uses controlled simulations and synthetically sampled sessions rather than human participants.

papers.title: Bibliometric-enhanced Systematic Literature Review of EEG in Education: Learning Concepts, Computational Methods, and Research Opportunities
papers.reason: It reviews human EEG research on attention and learning but does not itself implement an interactive task, eye tracking, qualifying EEG analysis, or cognitive generative modelling.

papers.title: EEG-Based Framework for Reflexive and Perceptual Assessment in CLIS: Preliminary Study in Healthy Volunteers
papers.reason: The healthy-volunteer pilot centrally measures frequency-specific steady-state EEG responses, but its passive sensory paradigms provide no interactive behavioral component.

papers.title: EEG-Based Cognitive Load Classification During Landmark-Based VR Navigation
papers.reason: Participants actively navigate landmark-based VR routes, but the EEG methodology is reported mainly through classifier performance without qualifying spectral or connectivity details.

papers.title: A Unified Cortical Circuit Model with Divisive Normalization and Self-Excitation for Robust Representation and Memory Maintenance
papers.reason: Excluded because it is a theoretical neural-circuit study without a human-participant population.

papers.title: Dissociating Cognitive Load and Stress Responses Using Single-Channel EEG: Behavioral and Neural Correlates of Anxiety Across Cognitive States
papers.reason: Human participants complete cognitively demanding auditory tasks, but the EEG analysis is summarized through individual frequency-band amplitudes rather than connectivity or richer frequency-domain methods.

papers.title: Sleep Brain and Cardiac Activity Predict Cognitive Flexibility and Conceptual Reasoning Using Deep Learning
papers.reason: It predicts human executive-function scores from passively recorded sleep EEG and ECG, using band power and deep classification rather than any qualifying task, connectivity analysis, or cognitive model.

papers.title: Evaluating Cognitive Assessment Tools:A Comparative Analysis of MMSE, RUDAS, SAGE, ADAS and MoCA for Early Dementia Detection
papers.reason: It reviews clinical cognitive assessment instruments for early dementia detection but contains none of the specified behavioral, ocular, EEG, or computational methodologies.

papers.title: Early Detection of Cognitive Impairment in Elderly using a Passive FPVS-EEG BCI and Machine Learning -- Extended Version
papers.reason: It targets human cognitive-impairment detection with passive EEG, but explicitly lacks behavioral interaction and does not describe qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Immersive Virtual Reality Assessments of Working Memory and Psychomotor Skills: A Comparison between Immersive and Non-Immersive Assessments
papers.reason: Human participants perform interactive digit-span, Corsi-block, and reaction-time assessments in VR and PC formats, but no eye tracking, qualifying EEG, or cognitive process model is included.

papers.title: Triangulating LLM Progress through Benchmarks, Games, and Cognitive Tests
papers.reason: Excluded because it evaluates the cognitive capabilities of LLMs using benchmarks, games, and tests designed for models.

papers.title: Interactive Virtual Games: Winners for Deep Cognitive Assessment
papers.reason: A clinical human sample completes interactive executive-function minigames with continuous behavioral and gaze-trajectory analysis for psychometric assessment and strategy profiling.

papers.title: Mazed and Confused: A Dataset of Cybersickness, Working Memory, Mental Load, Physical Load, and Attention During a Real Walking Task in VR
papers.reason: Participants actively navigate VR mazes and perform attention and working-memory tasks while eye tracking and continuous behavioral and physiological data are collected and analyzed.

papers.title: Pathformer3D: A 3D Scanpath Transformer for 360° Images
papers.reason: It centrally models human eye-tracking scanpaths from panoramic-image datasets, but the viewing condition is passive and the goal is scanpath prediction rather than cognitive assessment.

papers.title: Transcranial low-level laser stimulation in near infrared-II region for brain safety and protection
papers.reason: Human EEG and executive-function measures are secondary safety outcomes, with no described interactive paradigm or qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.
